title: Bound gas so transactions cannot get stuck
description: Avoid unbounded loops and storage growth that can push a transaction past the block gas limit and permanently brick a function.
body: Use when a function iterates over an array or mapping that users can grow. Do not loop over a list whose length attackers control, and avoid pushing all of a batch operation into one transaction. Actions: cap loop lengths, paginate large operations across transactions, and prefer a pull-payment pattern (users withdraw individually) over pushing to many recipients in one call. Validation signals include a gas test where the worst-case array size still fits under the block gas limit and a withdrawal path independent of list size. Risk boundary: an unbounded loop that grows past the block gas limit makes the function permanently uncallable (a denial of service that immutability cannot fix); treat user-growable loops as a blocking design flaw.
